phone_presence_notes: Handled a long, rambling, ADHD-by-admission customer with patience and genuine warmth — never showed frustration, matched her energy well, and the customer ended the call laughing and confident.
top_strength: Exceptional probing and booking close — Frank uncovered the full inventory across a chaotic 19-minute conversation (Murphy bed, workbench, extra appliances, bunk beds) and still secured the booking with a clear date, time, and realistic estimate range.
coaching_note: Frank never used the customer's name once across a 19-minute call — zero is a meaningful miss on rapport. The bigger gap is no differentiators: no damage coverage, no Meet Your Mover, no On-Time Guarantee mentioned despite a large, expensive move with a customer who'd used Einstein before. The 'you're in good hands' line was the closest to a value pitch. Adding the No Worries speech and Meet Your Mover mention would take this call from good to great.
